data.title: SentinelOne slides as BofA downgrades after 'mixed' Q1, guidance
data.description:
SentinelOne (NYSE:S) was in focus on Thursday after it reported mixed first-quarter results and guidance, prompting Bank of America to downgrade the cybersecurity company.

Shares fell 12.9% in premarket trading.

“The focal point of the last two quarters has been weaker guidance, with growth of 21%, -500bps off Street’s initial FY26 expectations,” Bank of America analyst Tal Liani wrote in a note to clients. “At the time, we interpreted this as a resetting of expectations by the new CFO. However, the company lowered its FY26 revenue growth guidance again this quarter from 23% previously to 22%, attributed to macro uncertainty and deal fluctuations. We also see weaker business trends, as [net new annual recurring revenue] declined -26% YoY in 1Q26 (vs -10% in 1Q25), and [net recurring revenue] ticked down a few points from an already low level of about ~110% last quarter, reflecting challenges to upsell.”

Liani lowered his rating on SentinelOne to Neutral from Buy, and cut his price target to $21 from $24.

And while the endpoint market is one of the most attractive in cybersecurity, SentinelOne is already benefiting from it, Liani said.

“We therefore believe the valuation is already reflective of the risks, yet the stock lacks upside catalysts,” he added.

SentinelOne said it expects revenue of $242M in the second-quarter, while sales for the full-year are expected to be between $996M and $1.011B. Analysts were expecting $244.87M for the second-quarter and $1.01B for the full-year.

data.title: Bearish sentiment rises after three weeks of fall, latest AAII survey shows
data.description:
Bearish sentiment among individual investors rose after falling for three consecutive weeks, showed the recent survey conducted by American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bearish sentiment as per the weekly AAII investor sentiment survey rose to 41.9% for the week ending May 28. This is compared to 36.7% in the prior update. Meanwhile, bullish sentiment as per the survey fell to 32.9% for the week, versus 37.7% last week.

Investors are treading carefully amid concerns over fiscal debt and the several twists and turns related to President Donald Trump’s tariff announcements.

Last week, Trump targeted Apple (AAPL) and said tariffs must be paid on iPhones not made in the U.S., while also threatening a 50% tariff on the EU. Later, he decided to hold off on imposing 50% tariffs on the EU until July 9 to allow more negotiations.

A trade court, on Wednesday, struck down the "reciprocal" tariffs that President Trump imposed on what he called "Liberation Day,".

The S&P500 (SP500), Nasdaq (COMP:IND) and Dow (DJI) all declined over 0.5% so far this week.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, showed a slight change during the week, with the figure standing at 25.2%, compared to 25.6% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Winshear Gold announces $500,000 private placement
data.description:
• Winshear Gold (TSXV:WINS:CA) announces a non-brokered private placement of up to 8.33 million units at $0.06 per unit for gross proceeds of $500,000.
  • Each unit comprises one common share and one half of one common share purchase warrant. Each full warrant will allow the holder to purchase one common share of Winshear Gold at $0.12 for a period of 24 months from the closing date of the financing. A finder's fee commensurate with regulatory policies may be paid if applicable.
  • Winshear Gold has entered into an agreement to acquire a 100% interest in the Thunder Bay Gold Project, located in northwestern Ontario. Proceeds from the financing will be used to advance the project and for general working capital purposes.  
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CIBC Q2 earnings beat, with strong net interest income, credit loss provision lower than expected
data.description:
Canadian Imperial Bank of Commerce (NYSE:CM) fiscal Q2 earnings exceeded the Wall Street consensus on Thursday as its net interest income came in higher than analysts expected and its provision for credit losses rose less than expected. Its capital markets and U.S. commercial banking and wealth management units turned in exceptionally robust Y/Y growth.

Q2 adjusted EPS of C$2.05 (US$1.48), beating the average analyst estimate of C$1.90, fell from C$2.20 in Q1 and increased from C$1.75 in last year’s Q2.

Revenue for the quarter ended April 30, 2025, dropped to C$7.02B (US$5.07B) from C$7.28B in the previous quarter and increased from C$6.16B a year ago. The current quarter topped the consensus of C$6.90B.

Adjusted preprovision pretax earnings of C$3.21B declined from C$3.42B in Q1 and rose from C$2.69B in Q2 2024.

CIBC (NYSE:CM) stock gained 1.5% in premarket trading.

Net interest income of C$3.79B, exceeding the Visible Alpha consensus of C$3.75B, was roughly flat with C$3.80B in the prior quarter and jumped from C$3.28B a year ago.

Provision for credit losses of C$605M, less than the Visible Alpha estimate of C$653M, increased from C$573M in Q1 and C$514M in last year’s Q2.

Adjusted return on equity of 13.9% vs. 15.3% in the prior quarter and 13.4% a year ago.

In March, CIBC (CM) said that Victor G. Dodig plans to retire as president and CEO, effective Oct. 31, 2025, and that Harry Culham was appointed as chief operating officer, effective April 1, 2025, and will succeed Dodig as president and CEO, effective Nov. 1, 2025

Adjusted net income by segment:

  • Canadian Personal and Business Banking: C$739M fell 4% Q/Q and increased 4% Y/Y.

  • Canadian Commercial Banking and Wealth Management: C$549M dropped 7% Q/Q and rose 13% Y/Y.

  • U.S. Commercial Banking and Wealth Management: US$125M slid 31% Q/Q and jumped 58% Y/Y.

  • Capital Markets: C$566M slid 9% Q/Q and grew 34% Y/Y.

Earlier, Canadian Imperial Bank Non-GAAP EPS of C$2.05 beats by C$0.15, revenue of C$7.02B beats by C$120M

data.title: Nvidia in spotlight as Wall Street praises 'very bullish' results, guidance
data.description:
It's Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) world, and we're all just living in it.

Wall Street firms seemed to run out of superlatives to describe how extraordinary Nvidia's stronger-than-expected results and guidance were, especially in light of the recent export controls to China.

Shares rose 6% in premarket trading, and were on track to regain the crown of most valuable company by market cap. Other semiconductor firms, such as AMD (AMD), Marvell (MRVL) and Broadcom (AVGO) also saw gains.

Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives described the quarterly results as “robust” and the commentary around demand as “very positive,” even when taking China into account.

“We continue to believe the path for Nvidia to a $4 trillion market cap and ultimately $5 trillion market cap over the coming years is on the horizon as this 4th Industrial Revolution plays out led by one chip in the world fueling AI … Nvidia,” Ives wrote.

Ives also noted that the recent deals from Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates are early signs of the sovereign and government demand that Huang has talked about. And with ongoing U.S.-Sino trade negotiations seemingly going well, Nvidia could recapture the $8B in lost revenue from the H20 ban and then some.

“This is a very important print and guide for the broader tech world and it shows the AI Revolution is heading into its next gear of growth despite the Trump tariff war playing out,” Ives added. 

Tech Stock Pros, Investing Group Leader for Tech Contrarians, said the results provided a “much-needed reset” after the China H20 ban. “The bottom line, however, is that Nvidia’s growth story is back on track after this print for at least the next two quarters,” they said via email.

With its latest results and guidance, Nvidia “fully” put digestion fears to rest and showed there is an acceleration of its business in every region, other than China, Morgan Stanley analyst Joseph Moore said.

“Everything should get better from here,” Moore added. He reiterated his Overweight rating on Nvidia and upped his price target to $170 from $160. 

Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya also raised his price target (going to $180 from $160) and said he sees long-term earnings power of $10 per share for Nvidia, as artificial intelligence demand now incorporates enormous demand from sovereign nations.

Jefferies analyst Blayne Curtis said it's “all systems go” for Nvidia, as the results showed supply challenges eased and some of the other segments that had been troublesome before — networking and gaming — returned to healthy growth. 

“The biggest issue for the stock was the disconnect between Blackwell sales and GB200 shipments but that is now in the rearview as NVDA noted multiple hyperscalers ramping 1k NVL72s/week,” Curtis wrote. Further, the second-half of the year is “shaping up nicely” for Nvidia, with “likely beat/raises from here,” Curtis added. 

What's more, the results are indicative that Nvidia is likely to remain best-in-class, J.P. Morgan analyst Harlan Sur said.

“Bottom line, the team continues to maintain a 1-2 step lead ahead of competitors with its silicon/hardware/software platforms and a strong ecosystem, and the team is further distancing itself with its aggressive cadence of new product launches and more product segmentation over time,” Sur wrote.

data.title: Merck, Daiichi Sankyo pull marketing application for lung cancer therapy
data.description:
Merck (NYSE:MRK) and Daiichi Sankyo (OTCPK:DSNKY) (OTCPK:DSKYF) announced Thursday the voluntary withdrawal of their U.S. marketing application targeted at the FDA’s accelerated approval of the lung cancer therapy patritumab deruxtecan (HER3-DXd).

The antibody-drug conjugate designed as a late-line option for adults with locally advanced or metastatic EGFR-mutated non-small cell lung cancer had previously been rejected by the FDA in 2024 due to regulatory concerns over a third-party manufacturing facility.

The companies said that their decision to pull the Biologics License Application (BLA) follows a topline readout from a Phase 3 trial for HER3-DXd and discussions with the FDA. It was not related to the FDA’s prior rejection of the drug or manufacturing issues, the duo added.

According to the companies, the confirmatory trial named HERTHENA-Lung02 for HER3-DXd in EGFRm non-small cell lung cancer didn't indicate statistical significance for overall survival, a key secondary endpoint.

data.title: Starbucks is downgraded by TD Cowen on profit concerns
data.description:
TD Cowen lowered its rating on Starbucks Corporation (NASDAQ:SBUX) to Hold from Buy as it lowered profit expectations for the coffee chain operator.

Analyst Andrew Charles and his team think Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) will settle into a 2026-2028 EPS base that is below a consensus expectation that does not appear to be considering labor investments central to the turnaround. The firm also sees a risk that a return to normalized same-store sales growth is delayed by "deteriorating value perceptions, historical underperformance in recessions, & increased competition."

TD Cowen's price target on Starbucks (SBUX) of $90 is based on an earnings multiple of 24X, which was noted to be in line with the five-year average off the two-year out EPS estimate.

Shares of Starbucks (SBUX) slipped 0.8% in premarket action to $85.31. The 52-week high is $117.46.

data.title: Eos Energy plunges after launching $75M stock offering
data.description:
Eos Energy Enterprises (NASDAQ:EOSE) -21.6% pre-market Thursday after saying it launched a public offering of $75M of common shares, with an underwriters option to purchase up to $11.25M of additional shares.

Eos (NASDAQ:EOSE) also will make a separate private offering of $175M aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2030, with an option to purchase up to an additional $26.25M of convertible senior notes.

The company said it plans to use the proceeds from the stock and note offerings to repurchase its outstanding 5%/6% convertible senior PIK toggle note due 2026 in privately negotiated transactions, prepay part of the amount due under its credit agreement with CCM Denali Debt Holdings, and for general corporate purposes.
